mechanism_narrative: A falling 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS lowers short-rate discounting and raises equity duration values; when the monthly change is negative, S&P 500 20-day forward returns should be positive, and when it is positive the sign should weaken or reverse.
failure_condition: Reject if the FEDFUNDS 3-month change has a nonnegative coefficient for predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% and CPIAUCSL keeps rising over the prior 3 months, restrictive policy and persistent inflation raise discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% while CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:2006-11 FEDFUNDS was 5.25 and CPIAUCSL vintage value was 201.7
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if the interaction coefficient is not negative at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening housing investment, falling construction employment, and weaker household balance sheets; when housing starts break down, S&P 500 60-day forward returns should be negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average and is down more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: fact:2006-10 HOUST vintage value was 1486.0 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdown triggers are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is still expanding and UNRATE remains at or below 4.6%, household income growth supports consumption and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 4.6% and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:2006-11 PAYEMS vintage value was 136018.0 and UNRATE was 4.5
failure_condition: Reject if this joint labor-strength condition has a negative walk-forward coefficient or if triggered 20-day returns are not higher than unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal consumer spending and near-term revenue support for index constituents; higher retail-sales momentum predicts positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2006-11 RSAFS vintage value was 368861.0 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS 3-month momentum coefficient is not positive in out-of-sample tests or if Rank IC remains below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in INDPRO and deceleration in PAYEMS signals weakening production and labor demand, reducing earnings expectations; the joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < 0 and PAYEMS 3-month change below its prior 12-month median
evidence_refs: fact:2006-11 INDPRO vintage value was 112.3227 and PAYEMS vintage value was 136018.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction term is not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months marks labor-market deterioration and recession-risk repricing; once this threshold is crossed, S&P 500 60-day forward returns should be negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses 0.3pp above its value from 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-threshold crossings are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if triggered drawdowns are not worse than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum falls while FEDFUNDS remains at or above 5.0%, investors price a future easing option without immediate recession evidence, creating positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses below its 6-month annualized rate while FEDFUNDS >= 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:2006-11 CPIAUCSL vintage value was 201.7 and FEDFUNDS was 5.25
failure_condition: Reject if the disinflation-with-high-rates trigger produces negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns fail to exceed baseline returns.
